name: git-client
title: Git Client
description: Powerful Git client for managing local repositories with intuitive interface
author: ernest0n

commands.name: recent-repositories
commands.title: Recent Git Repositories
commands.description: List of recently opened Git repositories

commands.name: open-repository
commands.title: Open Git Repository
commands.description: Open a Git repository management interface

preferences.name: defaultEditor
preferences.title: Default Editor
preferences.description: The default editor to open files with (e.g. 'Visual Studio Code')

preferences.name: defaultTerminal
preferences.title: Default Terminal
preferences.description: The default terminal app to open repository directory in (e.g. 'Terminal', 'Warp')

preferences.name: externalGitClient
preferences.title: External Git Client
preferences.description: The external git client to use for git commands (e.g. 'GitKraken')

preferences.name: commitsPerPage
preferences.title: Max Commits to Load
preferences.description: Number of commits to load per pagination page. Small values cause frequent lags during scrolling. Large values cause slow initial list loading.

preferences.name: maxBranchesToLoad
preferences.title: Max Branches to Load
preferences.description: Maximum number of branches to load in local and remote branches sections

preferences.name: aiCommitPrompt
preferences.title: AI Commit Message System Prompt
preferences.default:
You are a helpful assistant that generates concise, clear Git commit messages. Analyze the provided git diff and generate a conventional commit message that accurately describes the changes. Use conventional commit format: <short title>
<listed changes>. Focus on what changed and why.
preferences.description: System prompt used when generating commit messages with Raycast AI

scripts.prepublishOnly:
echo "

It seems like you are trying to publish the Raycast extension to npm.

If you did intend to publish it to npm, remove the \`prepublishOnly\` script and rerun \`npm publish\` again.
If you wanted to publish it to the Raycast Store instead, use \`npm run publish\` instead.

" && exit 1
